name: Multi Repetitor
description: Four-channel algorithmic rhythm and accent sequencer. Numeric, Zularic, or Euclidean banks feed four trigger outs and four accent outs; faders, switches, and tap tempo shape the groove.

controls.name: Prime
controls.description: Selects the base rhythm for the four lanes.
controls.detail: fader + CV · sets the prime rhythm

controls.name: Factor 1-4
controls.description: Sets the modification amount for each trigger lane.

controls.name: Set switch
controls.description: Selects the rhythm bank used as the starting point.
controls.detail: Left: Numeric · Middle: Zularic · Right: Euclidean

controls.name: Modify switch
controls.description: Selects how the Factor sliders alter the base pattern.

controls.name: Accent switch
controls.description: Chooses whether accent outputs are off or derived from neighboring lanes.

controls.name: Reset Tap button
controls.description: Taps tempo when free-running or resets when an external clock is present.

inputs.name: CLOCK
inputs.description: Clock input. Rising edges advance the four rhythm lanes.

inputs.name: RESET
inputs.description: Reset input. Re-starts the pattern on the next beat.

inputs.name: PRIME CV
inputs.description: Voltage control over the base rhythm selection.

inputs.name: SET CV
inputs.description: CV that scans across the rhythm banks.

behaviors.name: Three rhythm banks
behaviors.description: Numeric, Zularic, and Euclidean banks each define a different starting rhythm set for the four lanes.

behaviors.name: Lane modification
behaviors.description: Each lane can offset, multiply, or prime-shift its source rhythm independently, so one clock source can generate four related but distinct patterns.

behaviors.name: Accent derivation
behaviors.description: Accent gates are computed from neighboring lanes when enabled, so accents stay pattern-aware instead of acting like static velocity outs.

behaviors.name: Tap-tempo operation
behaviors.trigger: RESET TAP button with no external clock
behaviors.description: Repeated taps set the internal tempo. When an external clock is patched, the same button acts as a manual reset.

behaviors.name: Hands-free groove morphing
behaviors.trigger: CV into PRIME, FACTOR, or SET
behaviors.description: All rhythm-defining parameters can be voltage controlled, which lets the module move between locked patterns and evolving polymetric variations without touching the panel.

behaviors.name: Interface inversion
behaviors.trigger: invert orientation from the manual procedure
behaviors.description: The panel can be flipped for faders-down use, which keeps the performance layout usable in cases with reversed module rows.

patchIdeas.name: Four-voice drum grid
patchIdeas.patch: Euclidean bank on · Prime at noon · four trigger outs drive kick, snare, hat, and percussion · accents add motion to the same voices without extra sequencing.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Multi Repetitor:
* Set: Euclidean
* Modify: Multiply
* Accent: Next
* Prime: noon

Clock:
Kick:
Snare:
Hat:
Perc:

- Clock (Out) c> Multi Repetitor (CLOCK)
- Multi Repetitor (TRIG 1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Multi Repetitor (TRIG 2) t> Snare (Trig)
- Multi Repetitor (TRIG 3) t> Hat (Trig)
- Multi Repetitor (TRIG 4) t> Perc (Trig)
- Multi Repetitor (ACCENT 1) g> Kick (Accent)
- Multi Repetitor (ACCENT 2) g> Snare (Accent)
- Multi Repetitor (ACCENT 3) g> Hat (Accent)
- Multi Repetitor (ACCENT 4) g> Perc (Accent)

patchIdeas.name: Accent-to-timbre groove
patchIdeas.patch: Trigger lanes hit the drum voices while the accent outs open envelopes on a filter and VCA chain · the pattern stays simple, the motion comes from dynamic accents.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Multi Repetitor:
* Set: Numeric
* Modify: Offset
* Accent: Previous

Kick:
Snare:
Filter:
VCA:
Output:

- Clock (Out) c> Multi Repetitor (CLOCK)
- Multi Repetitor (TRIG 1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Multi Repetitor (TRIG 2) t> Snare (Trig)
- Multi Repetitor (ACCENT 1) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Multi Repetitor (ACCENT 2) t> VCA (CV)
- Kick (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Bank-scanned groove
patchIdeas.patch: A slow LFO scans SET CV while a stepped random source modulates the factor lanes · the core pattern keeps changing banks without losing the pulse.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Multi Repetitor:
* Set: CV-scanned
* Modify: Prime offset

LFO:
S&H:
Clock:
Drum:
Output:

- Clock (Out) c> Multi Repetitor (CLOCK)
- LFO (Out) >> Multi Repetitor (SET CV)
- S&H (Out) >> Multi Repetitor (FACTOR 1 CV)
- S&H (Out) >> Multi Repetitor (FACTOR 2 CV)
- S&H (Out) >> Multi Repetitor (FACTOR 3 CV)
- S&H (Out) >> Multi Repetitor (FACTOR 4 CV)
- Multi Repetitor (TRIG 1) t> Drum (Trig)
- Drum (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Manual reset fills
patchIdeas.patch: Let the pattern run, then hit RESET on the downbeat to force a fill restart · lane 4 becomes a predictable phrase marker while the other lanes keep turning over.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Multi Repetitor:
* Set: Zularic
* Modify: Offset
* Accent: Off

Clock:
Fill Gate:
Kick:
Hat:
Output:

- Clock (Out) c> Multi Repetitor (CLOCK)
- Fill Gate (Out) t> Multi Repetitor (RESET)
- Multi Repetitor (TRIG 1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Multi Repetitor (TRIG 4) t> Hat (Trig)
- Kick (Out) -> Output (L)
- Hat (Out) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Accent-driven modulation bus
patchIdeas.patch: Use the four accent outputs as a modulation bank · one lane opens a VCA, one hits filter cutoff, one shifts delay mix, one resets an LFO clock for a denser performance patch.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Multi Repetitor:
* Set: Euclidean
* Modify: Multiply
* Accent: Next

Filter:
VCA:
Delay:
LFO:
Clock:

- Clock (Out) c> Multi Repetitor (CLOCK)
- Multi Repetitor (ACCENT 1) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- Multi Repetitor (ACCENT 2) g> Filter (Cutoff)
- Multi Repetitor (ACCENT 3) g> Delay (Mix)
- Multi Repetitor (ACCENT 4) t> LFO (Reset)
- Multi Repetitor (TRIG 1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Multi Repetitor (TRIG 2) t> Snare (Trig)
